content.thinking: Let me start by checking the current state of the RabbitMQ cluster and then configure classic queue mirroring.

content.text: Let me first check the current state of the cluster.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-04-2026-2293b2c1-cluster-a get pods && kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-04-2026-2293b2c1-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_vhosts && kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-04-2026-2293b2c1-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app && kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-04-2026-2293b2c1-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy
input.description: Check current state of RabbitMQ cluster, vhosts, policies, and queues

content.text: The cluster is healthy with 3 nodes, /app vhost exists, app-queue is a classic queue with no policy. Now I'll set the `ha-all` policy on the /app vhost to mirror across all nodes.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-04-2026-2293b2c1-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_policy ha-all ".*" '{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}' --vhost /app --apply-to queues
input.description: Set ha-all mirroring policy on /app vhost for all queues
